Experimental value updated 10/22/13 from J. Peter Guthrie (University of Western Ontario) taken as a weighted average of the values -7.13 from a Cabani review, and -5.24 from experimental data from Parsons, Rochester, and Wood (see reference). The weighting gives preference to the latter since it gives source data.

Formaldehyde is covalently hydrated in water. Our value here is the true free energy of hydration (based on the concentration of formaldehyde in gas vs in water) as opposed to the apparent one. See Guthrie, J. P., J. Am. Chem. Soc. 2000, 122, 5529-5538.
